title: Our Warming Earth: Understanding Climate Change
passage:
Our Warming Earth

Our planet Earth is getting warmer every year. This warming is called global warming. It is a big part of climate change. Today, the temperature of the Earth is higher than it was one hundred years ago.

Why is the Earth getting warmer? The main reason is human activity. People build big factories and drive millions of cars. These factories and cars burn oil, coal, and gas. When we burn these things, they make greenhouse gases. These gases go up into the sky. They trap the heat from the sun like a big blanket around the Earth. Another reason is that people cut down many trees. Trees are important because they clean the air and absorb carbon dioxide. Without trees, the Earth gets hotter.

Global warming causes many problems for our planet. First, the ice in cold places like the Arctic and Antarctica is melting fast. When the ice melts, it turns into water and goes into the oceans. This makes the sea level rise. Many cities near the beach might flood in the future. Second, the weather is changing. Some places have very hot summers, and other places have very heavy rains and storms. Animals are also in danger. Polar bears, for example, lose their homes because the ice is melting.

We can do simple things to help our Earth. We can plant more trees in our gardens and parks. We can also save energy at home. For example, we should turn off the lights when we leave a room. Finally, we can walk or ride bicycles instead of driving cars. If we work together, we can protect our world.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What is the warming of the Earth called?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 1 says: 'This warming is called global warming.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What do factories and cars burn?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'These factories and cars burn oil, coal, and gas.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'Trees are important because they clean the air and absorb carbon dioxide.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Which animal loses its home because the ice is melting?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 says: 'Polar bears, for example, lose their homes because the ice is melting.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: The Earth is colder now than it was one hundred years ago.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 1 states: 'Today, the temperature of the Earth is higher than it was one hundred years ago.' Higher temperature means it is warmer, not colder.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Greenhouse gases trap the heat from the sun.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'They trap the heat from the sun like a big blanket around the Earth.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Polar bears only eat fish to survive.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 mentions polar bears losing their homes, but there is no information about what they eat.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: People should leave the lights on when they leave a room.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'we should turn off the lights when we leave a room.'

questions.instruction: Answer the questions below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: What is melting fast in cold places?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'the ice in cold places like the Arctic and Antarctica is melting fast.'

questions.instruction: Answer the questions below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: What might happen to cities near the beach in the future?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 says: 'Many cities near the beach might flood in the future.'

questions.instruction: Answer the questions below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: What can people ride instead of driving cars?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'we can walk or ride bicycles instead of driving cars.'

vocabulary.meaning: sự nóng lên toàn cầu (the warming of the Earth)
khí nhà kính (gases that trap heat in the sky)
tan chảy (to turn from ice into water)
ngập lụt (to be filled with water)

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài (Tactics)

1. Kỹ thuật tìm từ khóa trực tiếp (Scanning): Đối với trình độ A1-A2, các câu hỏi sử dụng từ ngữ gần như giống hệt trong bài đọc. Hãy tìm các từ khóa nổi bật như 'Polar bears', 'one hundred years', 'bicycles' để tìm nhanh đoạn văn chứa câu trả lời.

2. Đối với dạng True/False/Not Given: Cần lưu ý các từ phủ định hoặc từ trái nghĩa. Ví dụ, câu hỏi ghi 'colder' nhưng bài đọc ghi 'higher temperature' (warmer), đáp án chắc chắn là FALSE. Nếu thông tin không được nhắc đến (như việc gấu bắc cực ăn gì), chọn NOT GIVEN.

3. Đối với dạng điền từ ngắn (Short-answer): Đọc kỹ giới hạn từ (NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS). Chỉ lấy từ ngữ trực tiếp từ trong bài đọc ra để điền, không cần thay đổi dạng của từ.
